title: tier0-2: run mine_gold_anchor.py over historical changesets, publish first survival number
description:
Given the CSV of historical changeset PR URLs from tier0-1, invoke the harvester to classify each by merge-survival (merged + survived 30 days = accept, closed-unmerged or reverted = reject) and publish the first calibrated success-rate number for the agent-driven changeset pipeline.

Steps:
  1. python scripts/mine_gold_anchor.py --source changesets --changesets data/agent_changesets.csv --target-count 50 --out data/gold_anchor.json
  2. Read data/gold_anchor.json: count accept vs reject = headline success-rate
  3. Write a markdown one-pager to docs/poc_tier0_report.md with the headline number + a few example PRs in each bucket

Requires: gh auth login already done so the harvester can hit the GitHub API for revert checks. No SDK / no Anthropic key / no executor access needed.
acceptance_criteria: docs/poc_tier0_report.md committed; data/gold_anchor.json validates against schemas/gold_anchor_entry.schema.json. Decision recorded: proceed to tier1 or stop and dogfood.
notes: Blocked in practice (not by dep graph) on the orchestrator under evaluation having >=50 merged agent-created changesets aged 30d without revert, plus a stable migration-workflow filter, plus someone with access populating data/agent_changesets.csv. See docs/tier0_skip.md. Consider deferring until those conditions hold.

title: tier0-1: pull a CSV of merged agent-created changeset PR URLs
description:
Get a list of >=50 PR URLs that were created by the agent-driven changeset pipeline under evaluation AND are at least 30 days old AND landed in MERGED or CLOSED state. The result feeds tier0-2 (the harvester).

The pipeline owner exports the list (any mechanism: SQL query, API export, manual collation) satisfying:
  - PR was created by an agent-driven changeset workflow (not a human-authored PR)
  - external_state IN ('MERGED', 'CLOSED')
  - merged_or_closed_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'

Save the result as data/agent_changesets.csv (one URL per line, optional 'pr_url' header). See docs/tier0_skip.md for the three conditions that re-open this work.
acceptance_criteria: data/agent_changesets.csv with >=50 PR URLs, OR a documented 'no production history yet, skip to tier1' result.
notes: Resolved as 'no production history yet, skip to tier1' - the acceptance criterion's escape hatch. data/agent_changesets.csv ships as a header-only stub; docs/tier0_skip.md documents the three conditions that re-open tier 0. Real next work is vj9.3 + vj9.4.
close_reason: Documented 'no production history yet, skip to tier1' per the acceptance criterion. data/agent_changesets.csv ships as a header-only stub; docs/tier0_skip.md documents three conditions that re-open tier 0. Real next work is vj9.3 + vj9.4.

title: tier1-3: eval driver + first end-to-end markdown report on real agent output
description:
Wire tier1-1 (diff puller) and tier1-2 (sandbox adapter) into a single driver script that takes a list of workflow instance ids and emits a markdown funnel report.

Pseudocode:
  1. For each workflow_instance_id:
     a. Run scripts/pull_changesets.py -> /tmp/eval/<id>/{repo, patch.diff, meta.json}
     b. Build a Recipe(dockerfile, build_cmd, test_cmd) appropriate to the project (one Recipe per migration_id, hand-authored for the POC; commit them as configs/recipes/<migration_id>.yaml)
     c. funnel = run_funnel(repo_path, recipe, adapters={sandbox: DockerSandboxAdapter(), anthropic: None}, stages=(diff_valid, compile_only, tests))
     d. Write result.json under runs/analysis/mig_<id>/<runner>/<variant>/<id>/result.json
  2. python -m migration_evals.cli report --run runs/analysis/mig_X/<runner>/<variant> --out docs/poc_tier1_report.md
  3. python -m migration_evals.cli iterator-report --run ... --out docs/poc_tier1_iterator.md (per-batch view if iterator_id is populated)

Skip Tier 3 (LLM judge) for the POC unless tier2-1 (AnthropicAdapter) lands first.
acceptance_criteria: docs/poc_tier1_report.md committed showing funnel rates (% diff-valid, % compile, % tests pass), failure-class breakdown, per-repo cost, generated from N>=10 real workflow instance outputs.

title: tier1-2: SandboxAdapter for tiers 1+2 (compile + tests inside a container)
description:
The funnel defines a SandboxAdapter Protocol (src/migration_evals/adapters.py) with three methods: create_sandbox(image, env), exec(sandbox_id, command, timeout_s), destroy_sandbox(id). Tier 1 (compile) and Tier 2 (tests) call these to run the recipes build_cmd and test_cmd inside a clean container.

Two viable implementations - pick one:

OPTION A (POC speed, ~half a day): Pure-Python SandboxAdapter that shells out to docker. Apply the diff locally on the eval driver machine, docker run --rm -v <repo>:/work <image> sh -c BUILDCMD. No production infra needed; good enough for a POC over 50-100 repos on a beefy box. Add as src/migration_evals/adapters_docker.py.

OPTION B (production, ~1-2 days): Wrap the agent pipeline's executor-task API. The Protocol maps cleanly:
  create_sandbox -> Execute(spec) returns handle
  exec(handle, cmd) -> Execute another spec with cmd; poll Status until terminal; fetch Logs
  destroy_sandbox(handle) -> no-op (executor cleans up after Status terminal)

Pick A for the POC unless the production infra path is required from day one.
acceptance_criteria: Running python -m migration_evals.cli run --config X.yaml --stage tests against a directory of repo+patch dirs from tier1-1 produces one result.json per repo with oracle_tier in {diff_valid, compile_only, tests} and per-tier cost recorded.
notes: Closed with Option A (Docker CLI shell-out). Adapter at src/migration_evals/adapters_docker.py, 14 unit tests + 1 opt-in live-Docker integration test in tests/test_adapters_docker.py, wired into runner.py via build_sandbox_adapter() factory keyed off adapters.sandbox_provider in YAML. Docs in docs/oracle_funnel.md #Sandbox backends. Legacy cli.py --repos path left on cassette-only (acceptance criterion targets --config path, which is now Docker-capable). End-to-end --stage tests against real agent diffs still needs vj9.3 (diff puller) to produce the input repo+patch dirs.
close_reason: DockerSandboxAdapter shipped + wired; tests green (244 pass, 1 intentional skip for live Docker). End-to-end against real agent output now depends only on vj9.3 + vj9.5.

title: tier1-1: build diff puller (artifact storage -> local patch.diff layout)
description:
Write scripts/pull_changesets.py that, given a workflow instance id (or a list), pulls the agent diff out of the agent pipeline's artifact storage and lays it out for the funnel.

The pipeline under evaluation must expose, for each agent run, the diff it produced and the base commit it was produced against. Implementation talks to whichever artifact-storage backend the pipeline uses (S3-compatible object store, blob storage, container registry, ...).

Output layout per workflow instance (matches what the funnel expects):
  /tmp/eval/<instance_id>/repo/             <- fresh clone at the migration's base commit
  /tmp/eval/<instance_id>/repo/patch.diff   <- the agent's output
  /tmp/eval/<instance_id>/meta.json         <- {repo_url, commit_sha, workflow_id, agent_runner, agent_model}
acceptance_criteria: Running scripts/pull_changesets.py with N instance ids produces N populated dirs; git apply --check patch.diff inside each succeeds for valid diffs.

title: epic: integrate the eval framework with a real agent-driven changeset pipeline
description:
End-to-end integration of this eval framework with an external agent-driven changeset pipeline so we can score agent-produced diffs (compile / tests / LLM judge) and measure long-term success via merged-PR survival.

Today the framework runs against synthetic fixtures and replay cassettes. To score real agent output it needs:
  - A diff puller that reads result.diff out of the agent pipeline's artifact storage
  - A SandboxAdapter implementation (Docker-shell or executor-task wrapper)
  - Optionally an AnthropicAdapter for the Tier 3 LLM judge
  - Pre-registered oracle/recipe/hypotheses files committed in this repo

Three tiers, each independently deliverable:
  Tier 0 (~2h): retroactive scoring of historical merged changesets via mine_gold_anchor.py --source changesets
  Tier 1 (~3-4d): pre-PR scoring loop (diff puller + sandbox adapter + funnel driver)
  Tier 2 (~1w): embedded as a workflow node so the agent's next iteration sees the verdict (CI feedback loop)
acceptance_criteria: All children closed; one published markdown report demonstrating end-to-end scoring on real agent output.

title: tier2-3: pre-register oracle/recipe/hypotheses + CI publication gate
description:
Stand up the publication gate so no headline number leaves the project unstamped.

  1. Author three artifacts in this repo:
     - configs/oracle_spec.yaml - which tiers are enabled, thresholds, etc.
     - configs/recipe_spec.yaml - the build/test commands per migration_id
     - docs/hypotheses_and_thresholds.md already exists; revise it with concrete pre-registered claims (e.g. "Java 8->17 success rate >= X% on corpus Y by date Z")
  2. Add manifest.json next to every committed run output dir, pointing at those three files
  3. Add a GitHub Action that runs:
       python -m migration_evals.publication_gate --check-run <run_dir>
     on every PR that touches a migration eval result. The gate fails if any result.json is missing oracle_spec_sha / recipe_spec_sha / pre_reg_sha or if any stamp does not match the committed files sha256.
  4. Once the agents prompt is versioned, add prompt_spec to the manifest and prompt_sha enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: A test PR that adds an unstamped or stale-stamped run.json fails CI; the same PR with valid stamps passes. .github/workflows/publication_gate.yml committed.

title: tier2-2: contribute eval node to the agent pipeline workflow
description:
Land a new node type in the agent pipeline's workflow runtime that runs after the changeset-creation node and:
  1. Reads the agent's result.diff from artifact storage
  2. Invokes the funnel via subprocess (or RPC to a sidecar container) with stages=(diff_valid, compile_only, tests, judge)
  3. Writes the FunnelResult.to_dict() back as a workflow variable named last_oracle_verdict
  4. Optionally: gate auto-publish of the changeset based on verdict.passed

This unblocks the CI feedback loop - the agent's next iteration reads last_oracle_verdict from prompt context and adapts (verdict.details carries stderr_tail, the failing tier name, etc).

Reference: docs/oracle_funnel.md '## CI feedback loop integration' section in this repo. The PR for this work lives in the agent pipeline's repo, not here - this bead's acceptance is the merged PR URL.
acceptance_criteria: PR landed in the agent pipeline's repo introducing the eval node; an integration test exercises the read/write of last_oracle_verdict end-to-end.

title: tier2-1: AnthropicAdapter for Tier 3 LLM judge
description:
The funnel defines AnthropicAdapter Protocol (src/migration_evals/adapters.py) with one method: messages_create(model, messages, system, max_tokens, **kwargs). Tier 3 of the funnel uses this to ask Claude whether the agent's diff matches the migration intent.

Implement against the public Anthropic API (or any compatible proxy) so this repo stays self-contained. Add as src/migration_evals/adapters_anthropic.py.

Must:
  - Pass the system prompt as a list-of-content-blocks with cache_control: ephemeral on the rubric block. The rubric is identical across trials so prompt-caching is critical for cost.
  - Return a dict shaped {content: [{type: text, text: <judgement>}], ...} - this is what src/migration_evals/oracles/tier3_judge.py expects.
  - Cost guard: refuse to run if a per-call budget would be exceeded; the recipe's oracle_spec can carry the cap.
acceptance_criteria: Funnel run with stages=(judge,) succeeds against >=5 repos and writes per-trial judge verdicts. Observed cost per call in the ~$0.02-$0.08 range (cache hits vs misses).
notes: Shipped Option 1 (build + mock tests). AnthropicSDKAdapter at src/migration_evals/adapters_anthropic.py wraps anthropic.Anthropic().messages.create with: byte-identical cache_control passthrough, pre-call worst-case budget guard (BudgetExceededError before any spend), post-call cost accounting from response.usage, and lazy SDK import (cassette users stay dependency-free). Wired into runner.py via build_anthropic_adapter() factory keyed off adapters.anthropic_provider. Added 'live' optional extra (anthropic>=0.40) to pyproject. Tests: 16 new (15 unit + 1 opt-in live). Live acceptance criterion (>=5 repos with cost in /bin/bash.02-0.08 band) deferred until creds are wired in CI.
close_reason: AnthropicSDKAdapter shipped; full mock-test coverage; live verification gated on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + MIGRATION_EVAL_ANTHROPIC_INTEGRATION=1.

value: Repo migration-evals is positioned as a vendor-neutral evaluation framework for prospect-facing proofpoints. Do NOT add proprietary system names, internal team identifiers, private repo paths, or production schema details to docs, code comments, or bead notes - even when accurate. Why: prior scrubbing commits removed vendor-specific framing; the repo reads clean to prospects evaluating the framework. How to apply: when integrating with any specific consumer system, document the integration shape generically (interfaces, layouts, contracts); keep vendor specifics in a private out-of-repo note.
